title: buccella lamia
intro: Buccella lamia, also known as the "Moonseed" plant, is a type of poisonous plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's seeds, in particular, contain a toxic compound called bulaccine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Buccella lamia, such as teas and infusions, have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, due to its toxicity, the plant should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
